exemplar.title: How can we distinguish between knowledge, belief, and opinion?

detailedExemplarData.title: How can we distinguish between knowledge, belief, and opinion?

strengths: Clearly identifies three specific real-world objects (evil-eye amulet, Hick article, âSuitsâ episode) with adequate contextualisation
Consistent, explicit references to the chosen IA prompt throughout the commentary
Uses appropriate citations (e.g., full bibliographic details for the Hick article) to support factual claims
Well-chosen objects that allow concrete links to knowledge, belief and opinion distinctions
Title and prompt are clearly stated, providing a coherent overall framing
weaknesses: Analysis often relies on broad assertions without unpacking how examples illustrate TOK concepts (justification, reliability, validity)
Discussion of the pilot episode of âSuitsâ is repetitive and lacks depth in epistemic analysis
Commentary on Hickâs article drifts into general statements about relativism rather than focused exploration of the prompt
Limited comparative synthesis across the three objectsâconnections remain mostly separate rather than integrated
Formatting inconsistencies in citations (duplicate URLs, HTML artifacts, inconsistent reference style) detract from professionalism
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition meets the descriptor for the 5â6 band: three objects and contexts are clearly identified and links to the IA prompt are explained with supporting evidence, yet the precision and depth of analysis are uneven. The student provides competent contextualisation and references, but fails to fully develop TOK concepts (justification, reliability) or synthesize the objects into a cohesive argument. Given the examinerâs objective total of 6 and the submissionâs mixed but generally proficient performance, a score of 6 best reflects the workâs strengths (clarity of object selection and framing, adequate referencing) while accounting for its weaknesses in analytical depth and presentation consistency.

annotations.comment: Several URLs appear twice (e.g., the Evil Eye origin link); streamline by removing duplicates and ensuring each source is listed once accurately.
The main title and prompt are clearly stated, providing a strong framing for the exhibitionâs focus on knowledge, belief, and opinion.
Consider adding a brief introduction linking the prompt to your selection criteria for the three objects, to guide the reader through your choices.
The image of the amulet is engaging; reference specific visual elements (color, design) in analysis to deepen the link to cultural belief.
Footnote hyperlink formatting in the Suits section is inconsistent; unify citation style for web sources and include access dates.
Footnote entries for Suits Wiki lack author credentialsâconsider evaluating the reliability of fanâedited sources when supporting epistemic claims.
The header âÑÐ¾Ð¸Ñ Ð¸ÑÑÐºâ appears as garbled textâremove or clarify this section heading to maintain professionalism and coherence.
The student clearly identifies the Evil Eye amulet as a specific cultural object, situating it in a real-world context and meeting IA prompt requirements.
Commentary here is largely descriptive of the amuletâs cultural meanings but lacks explicit analysis of how this illustrates distinctions between knowledge, belief, and opinion.
Footnotes contain unresolved HTML artifacts (âlt;supâ)âensure citation formatting follows a consistent style and remove extraneous encoding errors.

The claim âSuperstition mixed with cultural beliefsâ¦â is asserted without linking explicitly to TOK concepts such as justification or evidence; expand to show how belief here differs from knowledge.
When posing âIt begs the question of how belief is distinguished from knowledge,â the student should define these terms in TOK language and reference theory to deepen analysis.
The statement about amulet cracks fulfilling purpose is interesting but would benefit from evidence or scholarly reference on cultural psychology to support the claim.
Providing full publication details for Hickâs article strengthens contextâgood inclusion of journal, volume, issue and DOI.
The summary of Hickâs article is overly descriptive and lacks explicit connection to the IA prompt; focus discussion on how his views illustrate distinctions between belief, opinion, and knowledge.
The final paragraph ends abruptly; add a concluding link back to how Hickâs argument illuminates our prompt on knowledge vs belief vs opinion.
In the analysis of Hickâs view (page 5), the student should evaluate his claims against specific TOK concepts like âjustificationâ and âevidenceâ to strengthen the link.
The pilot episode of âSuitsâ is wellâchosen to illustrate mediaâs role in shaping belief versus knowledge; its specificity aids concrete analysis.
The discussion of distortion risks remains general; incorporate epistemic terms (e.g., reliability, validity) and specific examples from the episode to analyse belief vs knowledge.
When considering media fiction vs reality, cite specific scenes or dialogue to illustrate how misrepresentation shapes opinion rather than established knowledge.
The key question about public perception of misinformation is valuable but remains unanswered; develop this into a comparative analysis with the other two objects.
The paragraph on misinformation blends rich questions with assertions; interweave theory from TOK to show how media shapes belief/opinion boundaries.
The footnote for Andersonâs article is formatted correctly but remains unincorporatedâreference this research directly in your analysis to support claims.
Ensure all reference list entries follow a single style (e.g., APA); currently, date formats and punctuation are inconsistent across items.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
